content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcal PRACTTcEs C ourvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 7,2001
Anthony Manzoni
417 41 Woodhaven Drive East
Palm Desert, CA 92211
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-01-228
Dear Mr. Manzoni:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place. In addition, this
letter is based on the facts presented to us. The Commission does not act as the finder of
fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) In addition, please note
that our advice is limited to the Political Reform Act. You may want to seek further
guidance from your county counsel or the Attorney General's Office regarding other
provisions of the law such as Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTION
Are you prohibited from purchasing an interest in the lease between College of
the Desert and the current owner of the driving range that is used by the college, your
employer?
CONCLUSION
No. The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not prohibit you from
purchasing an interest in the lease. The Act generally prohibits a public official who has
a financial interest in a decision from making, participating in making, or attempting to
use his or her official position to influence that decision.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
tl€lsB


File No. I-01-228
Page No. 2
FACTS
You have been a full-time tenured faculty member of the College of the Desert for
the past sixteen years as a professor and advisor to the golf management program atthe
college. Your students utilize the college driving range as part of your program.
You have been given an opportunity to purchase an interest in the lease between
the College of the Desert (lessor) and the current owner (lessee). The lease provides that
the golf management program may utllize the driving rarrge facility at no charge to the
college or the program, and the students.
The family of the new lessee, Victor Lobue, Jr. made a $500,000.00 contribution
in the creation of the golf management program. Mr. Lobue has invited you to
participate in the lease as a partner and watchdog for your program, to insure full usage
of the facility, pursuant to the terms of the lease. You will continue your role as a full-
time faculty member, and teach your classes in accordance with your collective
bargaining agreement and the rules of education. You would like to take advantage of
the opportunity to be a part of the lease of the golf facility. You would at no time or
whatever the situation, be a part of any negotiations that would in any way change the
conditions of the existing lease agreement, or for thatmatter, be a part of any attempt to
do anything to benefit yourself or your potential partners in the lease at the expense of the
college or the students. Your presence would insure fulI access and free usage to your
students for the remaining years of the lease.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials "should
perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial
interests or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." ($ 81001, subd.
(b).) Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating
in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has afinancial interest A public official has a "financial
interest" in a governmental decision within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the governmental decision will have a material firtancial effect on one or
more of the public official's economic interests. (g 87103;Regulation 18700(a).)
The Commission has adopted a standard analysis for deciding whether an
individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a given governmental decision. To
determine whether a given individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest under the
Political Reform Act:
1. Determine whether the individual is a public official, within the meaning of
the Act.
2. Determine whether the public official will be making, participating in making,
or using or attempting to use hisiher official position to influence a
government decision.


File No. I-01-228
Page No. 3
3. Identify the public official's economic interests.
4. For each of the public official's economic interests, determine whether that
interest is directly or indirectly involved in the governmental decision which
the public official will be making, participating in making, or using or
attempting to use his/her position to influence.
5. Determine the applicable materiality standard for each economic interest,
based upon the degree of involvement determined pursuant to California Code
of Regulations, title 2, section 18704.
6. Determine whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision
will have amaterial financial effect on each economic interest identified
pursuant to California Code of Regulations, title 2, section 18703.
7. Determine if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect is distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally.
8. Determine if the public official's participation is legally required despite the
conflict of interest. (Regulation 18700, subd's. (b)(1)-(8).)
As an instructor atthe College of the Desert you are a public official subject to the
conflict of interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048.) However, according to your
facts, you will not be participating in any decisions regarding the lease in question. So
long as you do not act or purport to represent your agency, the College of the Desert, you
are not restricted by the Political Reform Act from purchasing a portion of the lease.
However, you may not make, participate in making or influence a governmental decision
affecting the lease or Mr. Lobue, your partner.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General
By: Jody F
StaffCounsel
Legal
JF:jg

the Desert and the current owner of the driving range that is used by the college, your
employer?

CONCLUSION: No. The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not prohibit you from
purchasing an interest in the lease. The Act generally prohibits a public official who has
a financial interest in a decision from making, participating in making, or attempting to
use his or her official position to influence that decision.
